news_items.headline: TSMC boss frets about shortages of talent, water in Taiwan
news_items.summary: STORY: The head of the world's largest contract chipmaker, said on Friday that what his company lacks most is talent, while also worrying about water shortages. Taiwan's TSMC produces most of the advanced chips powering the AI revolution. But the industry has long complained about what executives call the "five shortages" ... water, power, labour, land and talent. Speaking at a rain-drenched ceremony for a new science park in southern Taiwan, TSMC CEO C.C. Wei said he was happy about the weather. In 2021, Taiwan imposed widespread water controls after the worst drought in its history. Wei also outlined how the government is trying to attract and retain foreign talent to help the tech industry. TSMC, a major supplier to Nvidia, is investing $165 billion to build factories in the U.S. state of Arizona. But the company has repeatedly said most of its production, along with research and development, will remain in Taiwan.
